data.title: As a fix-bug workflow, I can detect when a proposed fix would weaken existing test assertions
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add Gate 2c (test regression analysis) to fix-bug's post-investigation workflow. Analyzes the proposed fix to determine whether it would loosen, remove, or weaken existing test assertions. Integrates with Gate 1a's intent-confirmed result to suppress false positives on documented-intent corrections.
**Why**: Fixes that weaken test assertions enable future regressions. This gate distinguishes legitimate test updates (changing one specific value to another) from coverage erosion (broadening matchers, removing assertions, adding skip decorators). The 1a→2c interaction prevents false positives when a gate correction aligns with documented intent.
**Scope**:
- IN: Assertion analysis against fix proposal (pre-implementation); detection of assertion removal, specificity reduction (assertEqual→assertIn), assertion count reduction, skip/xfail additions; Gate 1a boolean flag read (intent-confirmed suppresses 2c for documented-intent gate corrections); PRIMARY SIGNAL
- OUT: Blast radius (Story 5); reversal check (Story 3); escalation routing (Story 6)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a fix that removes an assertion, replaces a specific expected value with a broader matcher, or reduces assertion count fires Gate 2c as a primary signal
  ← Satisfies: "A fix that removes a test assertion, replaces a specific expected value with a broader matcher...is flagged for user dialog"
- When this story is complete, a fix that replaces one specific expected value with a different specific expected value does not fire Gate 2c
  ← Satisfies: "A fix that replaces one specific expected value with a different specific expected value proceeds without interruption"
- When this story is complete, when Gate 1a has confirmed intent-aligned and the test change corrects a false positive against documented intent, Gate 2c does not fire
  ← Satisfies: "Gate 1a intent-confirmed suppresses false-positive gate firing on documented-intent corrections"
- When this story is complete, Gate 1a's three outcomes are terminal and immutable — Gate 2c reads the result as a boolean flag
  ← Satisfies: approach clarification on 1a→2c interaction
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← TDD Done-of-Done

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Test regression analysis operates on the proposed fix before implementation — must handle vague or incomplete fix proposals gracefully (fail open to dialog rather than auto-approving)
